name: AniBridge Downloader
overview: Download anime from multiple streaming sites directly within Jellyfin (English Sub/Dub only)
description: Search, browse, and download anime from multiple streaming sites (Anikoto, Anime Nexus) directly within Jellyfin. English Sub/Dub only. Adding a new site is a single pluggable adapter class.

versions.changelog:
v2.0.2.6
- Fix: Megaplay downloads still failing with the same ffmpeg exit 183 ("is not in allowed_segment_extensions") after v2.0.2.5 — that fix set upstream ffmpeg's "-allowed_extensions" option, but Jellyfin's own ffmpeg fork enforces a *separate*, additional segment-level check (a hardening patch from a past Jellyfin security advisory about malicious HLS playlists referencing arbitrary local files), which needed its own "-allowed_segment_extensions ALL" to be set. Both are now passed.

v2.0.2.5
- Fix: Megaplay downloads failing with ffmpeg exit 183 ("is not in allowed_segment_extensions" / "Invalid data found when processing input") — some of megaplay.buzz's backing CDNs (e.g. mewstream.buzz, flarestorm.buzz) serve HLS video segments disguised with unrelated extensions like ".jpg". ffmpeg's HLS demuxer rejects segment URLs whose extension isn't on its built-in safe list by default, so -allowed_extensions ALL is now passed to ffmpeg for every download.

v2.0.2.4
- Fix: Megaplay downloads still failing intermittently (ffmpeg exit 234, "Output file does not contain any stream" or "Stream map matches no streams") — the v2.0.2.3 fix of explicitly mapping -map 0:v:0/0:a:0 didn't hold up against every variant layout of megaplay.buzz's multi-program HLS master playlist. MegaplayExtractor now resolves the master playlist itself and picks the highest-bandwidth variant's own single-program media playlist up front, so ffmpeg only ever sees one program and its default automatic stream selection works reliably again (the explicit -map has been removed).

v2.0.2.3
- Fix: Megaplay downloads still failing after all retries ("ffmpeg exited with code 234: ... Output file does not contain any stream") — megaplay.buzz's HLS master playlist exposes each bitrate rendition as its own ffmpeg "program", which broke ffmpeg's automatic stream selection and left it mapping zero streams. ffmpeg is now told explicitly which video/audio stream to grab (-map 0:v:0 -map 0:a:0) instead of relying on automatic selection.

v2.0.2.0
- Fix: Anikoto episode titles were never populated (dropped during the v2.0.1.0 rewrite) — now filled from the API's per-episode title, falling back to "Episode N".
- Fix: Megaplay downloads failing after all retries ("ffmpeg exited with code N") — megaplay.buzz's CDN checks Referer on the actual video/segment requests, which ffmpeg wasn't sending. Extractors can now declare a required Referer that gets passed to ffmpeg.
- Improvement: ffmpeg failures now include the last few lines of ffmpeg's own stderr in the error message instead of just the bare exit code.

v2.0.1.0
- Fix: Anikoto "Failed to load series"/missing thumbnails — GetSeriesInfoAsync was calling a /api/anime/:slug endpoint that belongs to a different, unrelated community project and 404s on the actual deployed wrapper API. Series metadata (title/poster/genres) is now carried through from search/browse results instead of a second API call.
- Fix: Anikoto search/browse/episode parsing corrected against live API responses (search results' "slug" field includes a "/ep-N" suffix that must be stripped; episodes' "server_ids" is a single opaque string, not an array).
- Fix: Anikoto stream resolution — the API's /api/stream endpoint returns a megaplay.buzz embed page, not a direct video file. Added a MegaplayExtractor (decodes the real HLS URL from the embed page) instead of treating the response as a directly downloadable stream.
- Change: Only Anikoto's "HD-*" server is offered as a download option for now — VidPlay/Vidstream/VidCloud resolve through other embed hosts this plugin doesn't have a verified extractor for yet.
- Fix: Episode filenames/duplicate-detection for flat-episode-list sites (Anikoto) now use the real episode number instead of falling back to a slug+timestamp name and mis-detecting every episode as "already downloaded".
